overview: Species Identification: The snake appears to be a Pinzón Racer (Pseudalsophis slevini), which is endemic to Pinzón Island in the Galápagos. iNaturalist Physical Appearance: It features a distinct black-banded pattern over a yellowish-gray body, commonly reaching around 35–50 cm in length. Reptiles of Ecuador Behavior and Habitat: These are diurnal, terrestrial snakes found in volcanic rock areas, dry forests, and grasslands. Reptiles of Ecuador Conservation Status: The Pinzón Racer is considered vulnerable and faces a high risk of extinction in the midterm future. Reptiles of Ecuador

Physical Appearance: It features a distinct black-banded pattern over a yellowish-gray body, commonly reaching around 35–50 cm in length.
Behavior and Habitat: These are diurnal, terrestrial snakes found in volcanic rock areas, dry forests, and grasslands.
